stories.headline: Trump Reiterates Threat to Destroy Iran as Deadline Nears
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump reiterated his ultimatum for Iran to agree to a ceasefire deal or face a major military escalation, as both sides kept up attacks in the sixth week of the war.

stories.headline: Ukraine, Russia kill several civilians in tit-for-tat attacks
stories.summary: At least seven people killed in Ukraine and three in Russia, a day after Zelenskyy renewed his Easter ceasefire offer.

stories.headline: Iran War: Trump 'Not At All' Concerned About Committing War Crimes | The Pulse 4/7
stories.summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: 
Wolf von Rotberg, J Safra Sarasin, Equity Strategist; Azadeh Zamirirad, German Institute for International and Security Affairs, Africa and Middle East Research Head.

stories.headline: War in Iran Drives Russian Oil Prices to a 13-Year High
stories.summary: Russian crude prices rose to the highest in more than 13 years as Moscow benefited from the Iran-linked global oil rally.

stories.headline: Can Africa tackle the oil shock from the Iran war?
stories.summary: African nations are scrambling to secure oil and gas as the Iran war disrupts supplies from the Middle East.

stories.headline: How Pakistan’s solar boom is shielding it from worst of Iran war crisis
stories.summary: A quarter of Pakistani households are now using solar panels. This insulates millions of families from the energy crisis

stories.headline: How the Iran war and AI are making tech more expensive
Markets send mixed signals ahead of Trump's deadline to escalate Iran war
stories.summary: European and Asian markets sent mixed signals on Tuesday in anticipation of US President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to agree to a deal "before all Hell will reign down on them".

stories.headline: Palestine weekly wrap: Protests sweep West Bank after death penalty law
stories.summary: Israeli strikes on Gaza escalate with children among the injured and civilians killed in densely populated areas.

stories.headline: Russia provided Iran with intelligence on Israeli energy sites, Ukraine says
stories.summary: Kyiv stated that Moscow is sharing its satellite intelligence with Iran to help Tehran strike Israel’s energy sites, which could cause power grid collapse and mass blackouts.

stories.headline: Iran war and energy supply disruptions accelerate South Caucasus collaboration
stories.summary: When it comes to connecting the continents of Asia and Europe, Azerbaijan and Georgia play a vital role in the transit of energy. The leaders of both countries have outlined how they will work together to broaden the Middle Corridor.

stories.headline: Australia's most-decorated living soldier charged over alleged war crimes
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith, who denies all wrongdoing, previously lost a landmark defamation case over the alleged murders.

stories.headline: Trump Doubles Down on Iran Deadline; Universal Music Acquisition Proposal | Bloomberg Brief 4/7/2026
stories.summary: US equity futures and oil fluctuate as President Trump’s deadline for Iran to make a peace deal approaches. Bill Ackman's Pershing Square proposes an acquisition of Universal Music Group that would value the record label close to $65 billion. Sitara Sundar of JPMorgan Private Bank discusses her market outlook amid geopolitical uncertainties. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Three gunmen 'neutralised' in shooting incident near Israeli consulate in Istanbul
stories.summary: Authorities said a comprehensive investigation is being carried out with law enforcement units under the coordination of the chief public prosecutor's office.

stories.headline: JD Vance arrives in Hungary to back Orbán's re-election bid
stories.summary: The US vice-president's visit is the latest show of White House support for the Hungarian leader.

stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader visits China for expected meeting with Xi Jinping
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun said she "gladly accepted" Xi Jinping's invitation and hopes to be a "bridge for peace".

stories.headline: India's high-growth economy gets a Middle East oil shock
stories.summary: India's currency, stocks and growth projections take a beating as the country faces a triple energy shock due to Iran war.

stories.headline: McEwen Copper in Talks With Lenders to Fund Argentina Project
stories.summary: McEwen Copper Inc. is in talks with global lenders as it seeks financing for its $4 billion Los Azules project in Argentina, a potential step toward bringing one of the country’s largest undeveloped deposits into production.

stories.headline: Argentina Looks to Global Debt Markets to Finance Energy Boom
stories.summary: Argentina’s corporate borrowers are turning to global debt markets with a different goal this year — funding an energy-driven expansion rather than patching up balance sheets battered by years of crisis.

stories.headline: Bond Traders Risk Being Wrongfooted by 2022 Playbook, UBS Warns
stories.summary: Traders risk being wrongfooted by bets that major central banks will move in unison to respond to the threat of prolonged war in Iran, according to Bhanu Baweja, chief strategist at UBS Group AG.

stories.headline: Trafigura Fraudster Gupta Linked to Mauritian Bank Collapse
stories.summary: The businessman who masterminded a huge nickel fraud against Trafigura Group was named by Mauritius as the ultimate beneficiary of toxic loans that led to the collapse of a local lender.

stories.headline: What to Watch in the Election to Succeed Marjorie Taylor Greene in Georgia
stories.summary: Clay Fuller, a Republican allied with President Trump, will face Shawn Harris, a Democrat, in the election to fill the remainder of Ms. Greene’s term after her resignation from Congress.

stories.headline: Music giant Universal gets $64bn takeover offer
stories.summary: The music giant behind acts such as Taylor Swift and Sabrina Carpenter gets an offer from Bill Ackman's Pershing Square.

stories.headline: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
stories.summary: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.

stories.summary: Proposed transaction would combine world’s largest music group with a blank-cheque company

stories.headline: The future of global trade won’t depend on the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: A new infrastructure is being built rapidly to avoid the strategic chokepoint and ensure global energy and food security

stories.headline: JPMorgan to build Canary Wharf’s tallest tower after City airport approval
stories.summary: Bank wins clearance for 265-metre skyscraper after talks over low-flying aircraft

stories.headline: Student loan interest rates on controversial plans to be capped at 6%
stories.summary: Limit will apply to all plan 2 loans as well as plan 3 postgraduate loans

stories.headline: Citi sets aggressive targets for bankers in wealth management unit
stories.summary: Revised goals involve net revenue and value of assets clients entrust to Citi as business has lagged Wall Street peers

stories.headline: Brazil Courts Investors Ahead of Potential Euro Bond Return
stories.summary: Brazil is sounding out investors as it weighs a return to global debt markets, potentially paving the way for its first euro-denominated issuance in more than a decade.

stories.headline: BlackRock, State Street Target Invesco’s $379 Billion Tech Grip
stories.summary: Wall Street’s biggest ETF issuers are circling Invesco Ltd.’s Nasdaq 100 franchise, threatening to end its near-exclusive hold on the index.

stories.headline: US Premarket Movers: Broadcom, CVS, Estée Lauder, Wingstop
stories.summary: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.5% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York as traders await President Donald Trump’s Tuesday evening deadline for Iran to make a peace deal.

stories.headline: Mozambique Dollar Bonds Slump to 2023 Low on Restructure Plan
stories.summary: Mozambique’s dollar bonds fell to the lowest in almost three years after authorities gave the strongest signal yet of plans to seek restructuring talks with creditors.

stories.headline: Blackstone Hits $10 Billion Cap for Opportunistic Credit Fund
stories.summary: Blackstone Inc. raised $10 billion for its latest opportunistic credit fund, signaling sustained appetite from institutional investors to capitalize on upheaval in the private debt market.

stories.headline: Bank Indonesia Prioritizes Stability as Rupiah Hits Record Lows
stories.summary: Indonesia’s central bank is continuing to intervene in the markets with stability its “top priority” following the currency’s three-day slide to multiple record lows against the dollar.

stories.headline: Trump Calls Artemis II Astronauts After Their Historic Journey Around the Moon
stories.summary: The conversation celebrated a small, but significant, step in an ambitious plan for missions to the moon and Mars that Mr. Trump had set early in his first term.

stories.headline: Iran releases on bail Japan national jailed amid antigovernment protests
stories.summary: The person released is believed to be Japanese broadcaster NHK's Tehran bureau chief, Shinnosuke Kawashima.

stories.headline: Japan has enough oil to last beyond this year, says PM Takaichi

ASEAN survey finds growing doubts over US on trade and security

stories.summary: Fresh data from the Central Eurasia Venture Forum in Tashkent points to rising startup funding, AI investment and cross-border activity as governments move to improve access to capital and support market expansion.

stories.headline: Japan enacts largest state budget in history worth €664 billion
stories.summary: The upper house of the Japanese parliament has enacted a state budget of ¥122.3 trillion (€664bn), the largest allocation in the country's history. The move reflects PM Takaichi's goal of an expansionary fiscal policy.

stories.headline: Robot taxes, four-day work week: Inside OpenAI’s plan for an AI-driven economy
stories.summary: The company believes that AIs will soon become smarter than humans, which will have a drastic effect on how people work, live and pay taxes.

stories.headline: Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
stories.summary: The Israeli strikes reportedly targeted Hamas security personnel after they clashed with an Israeli-backed militia near Maghazi camp.

stories.headline: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.summary: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.headline: Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
stories.summary: The US president wants Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route, before his Tuesday deadline.

stories.headline: Oil Edges Higher in Choppy Trading Before Trump’s Iran Deadline
stories.summary: Oil bounced between gains and losses in a volatile session, with a deadline looming for Iran to meet US President Donald Trump’s demand to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face the destruction of key infrastructure.

stories.headline: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.summary: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

stories.headline: Trump's deadline looms but Asian nations already have deals with Iran
stories.summary: Nations in the region have been keen to reach agreements as their economies are heavily reliant on Middle East energy.

stories.headline: US Supreme Court paves way for dismissal of Steve Bannon conviction
stories.summary: The order allows a lower court to consider dismissing the former Trump strategist's indictment.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Clears the Way for Dismissal of Steve Bannon’s Conviction
stories.summary: Stephen K. Bannon, a former close aide to President Trump, was convicted for failing to comply with a congressional subpoena related to the investigation into the Jan. 6 attack.

stories.headline: When Suzuki met Suzuki: why a Tokyo dating agency is matching couples with the same name
stories.summary: Japan’s ban on married couples having different surnames has prompted an event to highlight people’s reluctance to change their nameAt the very least, the three men and three women calming their nerves on a Friday evening at a venue in Tokyo know they have one thing in common.Spaced out across booths, they will soon be placed in pairs and given 15 minutes to get to know one another. stories.headline: MAK Tells Evotec to List US Unit Worth Over €1 Billion
stories.summary: Activist fund MAK Capital, led by Michael A. Kaufman, has urged the leadership of German biotech firm Evotec SE to list its US unit and speed up its cost cutting program after the shares have lost about 85% over the past five years.

stories.headline: Shots Fired at Indianapolis Councilman’s Home, After Vote Backing Data Center
stories.summary: No one was injured, but the councilman, Ron Gibson, called it “deeply unsettling.”

stories.headline: The California Lake Billed as the ‘Saudi Arabia of Lithium’
stories.summary: Residents of Imperial County, Calif., are in dire need of an economic boost. Experts say the answer lies beneath the Salton Sea, where a lithium trove sits.

stories.headline: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
stories.summary: It comes a week before votes are cast and follows warnings of so-called false flag operations blamed on Ukraine.

stories.headline: Spain's huge pork industry seeks salvation from swine fever threat
stories.summary: Countries around the world, including the US, have already stopped imports over the outbreak.

stories.headline: Russia chose 'Easter escalation' over ceasefire, says Zelensky
stories.summary: Six civilians were killed and 40 others injured as Russia launched hundreds of drones and missiles.

stories.headline: AI videos fuel rhetoric as Orbán bids for four more years in Hungary
stories.summary: Videos have targeted Viktor Orbán's election rival, who could unseat him after 16 years in office.

stories.headline: Faced with new energy shock, Europe asks if reviving nuclear is the answer
stories.summary: As war drives up gas and fuel prices, Europeans turn again to the issue of energy independence.

stories.headline: Watch: Who is the coup leader who will be Myanmar's next president?
stories.summary: Min Aung Hlaing presided over his last military parade in Myanmar before he takes up the role of president.

stories.headline: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.summary: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.headline: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
stories.summary: Quake with epicentre west-north-west of Ternate island shakes cities and prompts regional tsunami warningOne person has been killed after a 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Indonesia’s Ternate island, damaging buildings and triggering small tsunami waves.The quake, which had a depth of 35km, occurred on Thursday at 6.48am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey. Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an

stories.headline: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
stories.summary: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

stories.headline: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
stories.summary: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

stories.headline: Macron calls Trump's remarks on his marriage 'inelegant'
